Top 3 ramen spots in Tokyo? 🗼🍜

#ramen #ramenrestaurant #japan #japanesefood #shinjuku #goldengai #japaneseculture #ramenlovers #japantravel #japantrip #japanlife #shibuya #japanfood #japanese
transcript: After trying over 400 bowls of ramen in Tokyo, what are your personal top three favorites? Let's go down the line. All right, number one, Ramen Shima here in Shinjuku. This place, they only serve about 60 bowls a day. You're gonna have to line up around eight in the morning, write your name down on the list, and then come back at the time that it says, but you're gonna get a real good classic shoyu shio ramen, real legit, real gourmet. Kind of worth it if you put the effort in. Number two, we got Kikanbo over in Kanda, other side of town. This is like the spicy miso shop. You can choose your level of spice. You know, Japan doesn't really do spice, but here they do, so be careful. If you get the devil level, you're done. Number three, you know what? I'm gonna say Nagi, Golden Guy. So Golden Guy is this bar part of town, packed, hundreds of little bars, and there's a ramen shop open 24 seven there. Does a Neboshi ramen, really strong, fishy ramen. But it's in a cool part of town. You're probably gonna be checking that area anyways. Might as well get a bowl at Nagi.
